sections.paragraph:
ἐγκαταλείπω impf. ἐγκατέλειπον; fut. ἐγκαταλείψω; 1 aor. 3 pl. ἐγκατέλειψαν (TestJob 43:10); 2 aor. ἐγκατέλιπον, subj. ἐγκαταλίπω; perf. ἐγκαταλέλοιπα LXX. Pass.: 1 fut. ἐγκαταλειφθήσομαι; 1 aor. ἐγκατελείφθην; perf. 3 sg. ἐγκαταλέλειπται Ps 9:35, ptc. ἐγκαταλελειμμένος LXX, inf. ἐγκαταλελεῖφθαι (s. prec. entry; Hes., Hdt.+)

① to cause someth. to remain or to exist after a point in time, leave of posterity Ro 9:29 (Is 1:9.—Cp. Lucian, Dial. Deor. 25, 1 εἰ μὴ ἐγὼ … , οὐδὲ λείψανον ἀνθρώπων ἐπέμεινεν ἄν).

② to separate connection with someone or someth., forsake, abandon, desert (Socrat., Ep. 14, 10 [Malherbe p. 258] of soul and body; SIG 364, 88; 97; 495, 135 [III b.c.]; UPZ 71, 8 [152 b.c.]; POxy 281, 21; PTebt 27, 16; LXX; TestJos 2:4) τινά someone (X., Cyr. 8, 8, 4; Polyb. 3, 40, 7; Diod S 11, 68, 3; 18, 7, 6; Appian, Mithrid. 105 §493 desert one who is in danger; Jos, Vi. 205) 2 Ti 4:10, 16. Of feeling or being forsaken by God (TestJos 2:4) Mt 27:46; Mk 15:34 (both Ps 21:2; cp. Billerb. II 574–80; Dalman, Jesus [tr. 204–7]; WHasenzahl, D. Gottverlassenh. des Christus … u. d. christolog. Verständnis des griech. Psalters ’37; FDanker, ZNW 61, ’70, 48–69 [lit.]); 2 Cor 4:9; Hb 13:5 (Josh 1:5; Dt 31:6, 8; 1 Ch 28:20); B 4:14; Hm 9:2 (as Dt 31:6, 8); Hs 2:9; 1 Cl 11:1; abandon the fountain of life B 11:2 (Jer 2:13); God’s commandments D 4:13; B 19:2; ἐ. τὴν ἀγάπην forsake love 1 Cl 33:1 (Dio Chrys. 57 [74], 8 τ. φιλίαν; Jos., Ant. 2, 40 τ. ἀρετήν).—Cease assembling Hb 10:25 (do or carry on someth. in a negligent manner, be remiss is also prob.: Diod S 15, 9, 1 τὴν πολιορκίαν; Mitt-Wilck. I/2, 72, 8ff μηδένα δὲ τῶν ἱερέων ἢ ἱερωμένων ἐνκαταλελοιπέναι τὰς θρησκείας).—Leave (Menand., Epitr. 550 τούτοις μή μʼ ἐγκαταλίπῃς), allow to remain (cp. Demosth. 57, 58) τὴν ψυχὴν εἰς ᾅδην the soul in Hades Ac 2:27 (Ps 15:10), 31 (for ἐ. τινὰ εἰς cp. PsSol 2:7).—DELG s.v. λείπω. M-M. Spicq.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pl. = plural

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

perf. perf. = perfect

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. sg. = singular

ptc. ptc. = participle

inf. inf. = infinitive

prec. prec. = preceding

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Malherbe Malherbe = AM., various works

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Billerb. Billerb. = HLStrack and PBillerbeck, Kommentar z. NT aus Talmud u. Midrasch—List 6

Dalman Dalman = GD., various works—List 6

  1. tr. = translate, etc.

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

Hs Hs = Similitudes

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

prob. prob. = probable, probably

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 273
